title: Java Inheritance Extends
description: Practice extends, super, and overriding a method.
learningGoals: Call a superclass constructor with super.

choices: car, the value passed to super
Compilation fails because Vehicle has no toString
completionChecks.explanation: Car overrides toString to return "Car", and an overriding method is chosen by the object's runtime type, so new Car().toString() is "Car".

completionChecks.prompt: Why does Car call super("car") in its constructor?
choices: Vehicle has no no-argument constructor, so Car must call super with a String
super is required only when overriding toString
It copies all private fields of Vehicle into Car
Compilation fails because a subclass cannot call super
completionChecks.explanation: Vehicle declares only Vehicle(String), so there is no implicit no-arg super to call; Car must invoke super("car") explicitly to initialize its superclass.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if String label = this.toString(); is placed before super("car"); inside Car()?
choices: Compilation fails because the constructor prologue cannot use this before delegation
The instance method runs, then Vehicle(String) runs normally
It compiles only because Vehicle.name is private
A NullPointerException is thrown before super runs
completionChecks.explanation: Java 25 flexible constructor bodies allow parameter-only validation before super(...), but pre-delegation code cannot read this or call instance methods on the object being constructed.
